title: Cholesterol
oneLiner: Cholesterol is a fat-like substance in your blood. Too much bad cholesterol blocks arteries and raises heart attack risk.

cards.title: Cholesterol is Not All Bad
cards.body: Your body needs cholesterol to make hormones, vitamin D, and digestive juices. The problem starts when levels go too high — especially LDL ('bad') cholesterol.

cards.title: LDL — The Bad Cholesterol
cards.body: LDL particles deposit cholesterol on artery walls, forming plaques. Over years, plaques narrow arteries, reduce blood flow, and can cause a heart attack or stroke.

cards.title: HDL — The Good Cholesterol
cards.body: HDL is like a garbage truck — it picks up excess cholesterol from arteries and takes it back to the liver for disposal. Higher HDL = lower heart risk.

cards.title: Triglycerides — The Hidden Danger
cards.body: Triglycerides are another blood fat. High triglycerides (from sugar, refined carbs, and alcohol) combined with low HDL is a common pattern in Indians — very dangerous.

cards.title: Saturated Fat Raises LDL
cards.body: Butter, ghee in excess, vanaspati, coconut oil used in large quantities, and fatty meat raise LDL cholesterol. Swap to olive oil, rice bran, or mustard oil in moderate amounts.

cards.title: Good Fats Lower LDL
cards.body: Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats (avocado, nuts, fatty fish, olive oil) actually lower LDL and raise HDL. Fat is not the enemy — the type of fat matters.

cards.title: Fibre is Your Cholesterol Mop
cards.body: Soluble fibre (oats, dal, beans, psyllium husk/isabgol) binds to cholesterol in the gut and carries it out of the body before it enters the blood. Eat more dal, oats, and sabzi.

doEat.name: Dal & rajma
doEat.note: Soluble fibre binds and removes cholesterol

doEat.name: Fatty fish (rohu, hilsa, mackerel)
doEat.note: Omega-3 lowers triglycerides and raises HDL

doEat.name: Isabgol (psyllium husk)
doEat.note: Soluble fibre, proven to lower LDL

dontEat.name: Vanaspati / dalda
dontEat.note: Trans fat — worst for LDL and HDL

dontEat.name: Coconut oil in large amounts
dontEat.note: High in saturated fat despite being 'natural'

lifestyle: Exercise for 30 minutes most days — aerobic activity raises HDL (good cholesterol) effectively.
Quit smoking — smoking directly lowers HDL and damages artery walls.
Replace refined carbs (white rice, maida) with fibre-rich alternatives to reduce triglycerides.
Limit alcohol — it is one of the biggest drivers of high triglycerides.
Get a lipid profile test at least once a year after age 30.
Manage weight — even 5–10% weight loss significantly improves cholesterol numbers.
redFlags: Chest pain, tightness, or pressure — especially during activity or climbing stairs
Pain radiating to the left arm, jaw, or back
Yellow fatty deposits around eyes (xanthelasma) or tendons — sign of very high cholesterol
LDL above 190 mg/dL on blood test — requires urgent medical review

quiz.q: Which type of cholesterol is called 'bad' because it builds up in arteries?
quiz.why: LDL deposits cholesterol in artery walls forming plaques — hence the 'bad' label.

quiz.q: High sugar and alcohol intake can raise triglycerides.
quiz.why: Excess sugar and alcohol are converted to triglycerides by the liver — a key driver of high triglyceride levels.

quiz.q: Which food actively lowers LDL cholesterol?
quiz.why: Oats contain beta-glucan, a soluble fibre that binds cholesterol in the gut and removes it from the body.

quiz.q: All fat is bad and should be avoided to lower cholesterol.
quiz.why: Unsaturated fats (nuts, fish, olive oil) actually lower LDL and raise HDL. It's trans fat and excess saturated fat that cause problems.

options: Transports fat to the liver to be removed
quiz.why: HDL acts like a garbage truck, collecting excess cholesterol from arteries and returning it to the liver for disposal.
